description_long: This shelter consists of a large, flat, rectangular roof structure supported by a single, small, box-like column at its center. The roof appears to be made of a solid, brown material, possibly wood or metal, and is expansive in relation to the supporting column. The central support is a compact, rectangular block with a metallic or concrete appearance, providing stability to the wide roof. There are no visible walls, enclosures, or side panels, making the shelter open on all sides. The design is minimalistic, with no decorative elements or additional features such as benches or railings. The structure is likely intended to provide overhead protection from sun or rain in public spaces such as parks, sidewalks, or transit stops.
description: A minimalistic, flat-roofed shelter with a single central support column and open sides, constructed from solid materials. The structure provides overhead protection in urban environments.
description_view_0: The flat, brown roof dominates the view, with the central support column visible as a small rectangle in the middle.
description_view_1: The shelter is seen from the narrow end, highlighting the small, boxy support column beneath the wide roof.
description_view_2: The long, flat roof is prominent, with the central support column visible as a low, rectangular block.
description_view_3: Similar to view 1, showing the narrow end and the central support column beneath the roof.
attribute_entryway_design: open on all sides with no defined entryway
